assertions.text: Narration does not describe what the picture already shows
Each narration block is labeled with approximate screen position (e.g., 'Over the departure shot')
Word count is noted for each narration block
Output includes a separate Commentary Notes section flagging where silence works better than words
No narration sentence exceeds 25 words without a natural internal break
Questions are organized into three labeled sections: Opening, Substantive, and High-Stakes
No question can be answered with a simple yes or no
Production notes appear on at least 3 questions throughout the set
High-stakes or challenging questions appear only in the final section, not in the opening
Output contains both an Original and Adapted version clearly labeled, with approximate word counts for each
All factual content from the original narration is preserved in the adapted version — no facts added or removed
Output includes a Voice Notes section (100-150 words) describing specific changes made, not just generic descriptions
Each scene is numbered and includes approximate running time, Action, Narration Cue, Interview Placement, and B-roll Required fields
Scene running times sum approximately to the target sequence length specified in the input
Output includes a Sequence Notes section that flags at least one missing material risk or structural problem, plus a Next Step note
Output uses two-column format with narration text and corresponding visual direction for every beat — no orphaned text without a visual cue
Narration word count is approximately 150 words per minute of target duration (within 15% tolerance)
Output includes deliberate pauses or beats, a Production Notes section, and the sequence opens/closes with connections to the documentary narrative
